sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: Admission to the Bachelor of Music is intended for especially qualified students who wish to emphasize professional training in performance within a four ‐ year program. Music majors are required to earn a minimum grade of 2.0 in each music course (core and elective) that is to count toward Music degree requirements. A GPA of 2.5 in Music is required for graduation. A minimum of 180 credits is required, of which at least 60 must be taken in departments other than the School of Music. These 60 credits may include courses required to satisfy general education requirements .

requirementCueLines: Admission to the Bachelor of Music is intended for especially qualified students who wish to emphasize professional training in performance within a four ‐ year program. Music majors are required to earn a minimum grade of 2.0 in each music course (core and elective) that is to count toward Music degree requirements. A GPA of 2.5 in Music is required for graduation. A minimum of 180 credits is required, of which at least 60 must be taken in departments other than the School of Music. These 60 credits may include courses required to satisfy general education requirements .
MUSIC 113: Beginning Ear Training ( Autumn ) (1 credit)
MUSIC 119: Theory ( Autumn ) (3 credits)
MUSIC 120: Survey of Music History ( Aut/Win/Spr ) (5 credits) or proficiency exam
MUSIC 201, 202: First-Year Theory I, II ( Winter, Spring ) (3,3) (6 credits)
MUSIC 204, 205: First-Year Ear Training I, II ( Winter, Spring ) (1,1) (2 credits)
MUSIC 203, 301, 302: First-Year Theory III & Second-Year Theory I, II ( Aut,Win,Spr ) (3,3,3) (9 credits)
MUSIC 206, 304, 305: First-Year Ear Training III & Second Year-Ear Training I, II ( Aut,Win,Spr ) (1,1,1) (3 credits)
MUSIC 210, 211, 212: Music History I, II, III ( Aut,Win,Spr ) (3,3,3) (9 credits)
MUSIC 303: Second-Year Theory III ( Autumn ) (3 credits)
MUSIC 306: Second-Year Ear Training III ( Autumn ) (1 credit)
At least 3 credits from among: MUSIC 250, MUSIC 251, MUSIC 252, or any 400-level Ethnomusicology course
TOTAL CORE CREDITS: 36
Additional Music Requirements
Approved upper-level Music Theory or History electives (see list of course options) (9 credits)
MUSIC 487: Tonal Counterpoint (3 credits)
One upper-level course from the following: MUSIC 425, 430, 438, 473, 474, 475, or 480 (3 credits)
MUSICP 321: Private Instruction (3) (18 credits)
MUSICP 421: Private Instruction (3) (18 credits)
MUSIC 326, 327, 328: Repertoire (2, 2, 2) (6 credits)
MUSIC 434, 435, 436: Pedagogy (2, 2, 2) (6 credits)
MUSIC 379: Junior Recital (1 credit)
MUSIC 479: Senior Recital (1 credit)
Music Electives (may not include performance) (7 credits)
Ensemble Requirements
Participation in at least one School of Music ensemble is required during every quarter of applied music instruction. (12 credits)
Choose from any choral ensemble, MUSEN 361 (Piano Ensemble), MUSEN 369 (Baroque Chamber Ensemble), MUSEN 383 (Collegium Musicum), or MUSEN 384 (Contemporary Group).
Piano majors are also required to take at least 6 quarters of MUSEN 325 (Accompanying).
TOTAL MAJOR CREDITS: 120
TOTAL NON-MUSIC CREDITS: 60
See the Degree Program Sheet below for a checklist of course requirements.

parsedDegreeMapBlockCandidates.title: Piano, B.M. parsed official source requirements
sourceLineHints: Admission to the Bachelor of Music is intended for especially qualified students who wish to emphasize professional training in performance within a four ‐ year program. Music majors are required to earn a minimum grade of 2.0 in each music course (core and elective) that is to count toward Music degree requirements. A GPA of 2.5 in Music is required for graduation. A minimum of 180 credits is required, of which at least 60 must be taken in departments other than the School of Music. These 60 credits may include courses required to satisfy general education requirements .
MUSIC 113: Beginning Ear Training ( Autumn ) (1 credit)
MUSIC 119: Theory ( Autumn ) (3 credits)
MUSIC 120: Survey of Music History ( Aut/Win/Spr ) (5 credits) or proficiency exam
